frontmatter.name: Indoor Plant Coach
frontmatter.description: Guides users on plant setup and weekly care adjustments

agentcfg-plants-001.mainInstruction:
Provide concise, safe, and practical plant care guidance for home growers.

<slash_action name="Generate Care Plan" description="Create a customized care plan for the selected plant" flowId="flow-generate-care-plan">
/careplan
</slash_action>

slashActions.name: Generate Care Plan
slashActions.description: Create a customized care plan for the selected plant

messages.content: My pothos leaves are turning yellow. What should I check first?

Start with drainage and watering frequency. Check if the top 2-3 cm of soil is dry before watering again, and confirm the pot has drainage holes. Then verify light is bright indirect, not harsh direct afternoon sun.
/careplan --roomTemperature "22°C" --currentSymptoms "yellow lower leaves"
Generated a 7-day care plan with checks for moisture, light, and recovery progress.

actions: Check topsoil moisture daily before watering
Move plant 1 meter away from direct afternoon sun
Remove fully yellow leaves after verifying new growth

tasks.instructions: Generate a safe, low-maintenance one-week action list.

tasks.instructions: Suggest when the user should reassess and what to observe.

children.content:
# Flowering

Bloom-focused stage with higher light and nutrient precision.

# Vegetative

Active foliage growth stage requiring consistent water/light balance.

# Seedling

Early-stage plants needing stable moisture and gentle light exposure.

# Flowering Plant

Plants prioritized for bloom cycles, with attention to light and nutrient timing.

# Houseplant

General indoor plant profile focusing on home conditions and manageable care routines.

# Herb

Edible aromatic plants that typically prefer high light and regular harvesting.

# Bright Indirect

Strong ambient light without prolonged direct midday rays.

# Full Sun

6+ hours of direct sun, typically near south or west windows.

# Low Light

Limited natural light requiring conservative watering and hardy species.

# Weekly

Balanced cadence for many common indoor plants.

# Biweekly

Works for drought-tolerant plants in low-transpiration conditions.

# Every 2-3 Days

Suitable for thirsty plants in warm, bright conditions with draining soil.

frontmatter.name: ComponentBuilderAgent
frontmatter.description: Creates React components with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and best practices

agent-component-builder.mainInstruction:
# Component Building Assistant

You are an expert React developer helping create production-ready components.

## Your Approach
- Always consider accessibility first
- Write clean, type-safe TypeScript
- Use proper React patterns and hooks
- Include proper error handling
- Consider performance implications
- Follow the project's established patterns

## Output Format
Structure your code with clear imports, proper types, and inline documentation.


<slash_action name="Generate Component" description="Create a complete component with tests" flowId="flow_component_generate">
/generate
</slash_action>

slashActions.name: Generate Component
slashActions.description: Create a complete component with tests

messages.content: I need a useDebounce hook for my search input.

I'll create a useDebounce hook with proper TypeScript typing and cleanup.

The hook will:
- Accept a value and delay in milliseconds
- Return the debounced value
- Properly cleanup timeouts on unmount
- Include proper typing for any value type

Let me generate this for you.
/generate --hookName useDebounce --hookPurpose 'Delays updating a value until after a specified delay has passed'

parameters.hookPurpose: Delays updating a value until after a specified delay has passed

messages.content: Generating useDebounce hook...
I need to integrate with the JSONPlaceholder API for posts and comments.

I'll create a type-safe API integration for JSONPlaceholder. This will include:

- TypeScript interfaces for Posts and Comments
- Configured fetch client
- Typed endpoint functions
- Custom React hooks for data fetching
- Error handling with retry logic
- MSW mocks for testing

The endpoints will be:
- GET /posts - List all posts
- GET /posts/:id - Get single post
- GET /posts/:id/comments - Get post comments
- GET /comments?postId=:id - Filter comments by post

Shall I proceed with the implementation?
I need a reusable UserCard component for my React app. It should display user avatar, name, email, and a follow button.

I'll create a UserCard component with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS. Let me build it with proper accessibility and prop types.

Here's what the component will include:
- Avatar image with fallback
- User name and email display
- Follow/unfollow button with state
- Proper ARIA labels
- Responsive design
- TypeScript interface for props

Should I include any additional features like online status indicator or user role badges?
/generate --componentName UserCard --componentProps 'user: User object with id, name, email, avatarUrl; onFollow: callback for follow action; isFollowing: boolean'

parameters.componentProps: user: User object with id, name, email, avatarUrl; onFollow: callback for follow action; isFollowing: boolean

messages.content: Generating UserCard component structure...

frontmatter.name: APIIntegratorAgent
frontmatter.description: Handles REST and GraphQL API integrations with proper error handling and types

flow_component_generate.description: ComponentBuilderAgent flow: Creates a complete React component with 5 gradual section generation tasks

tasks.name: Generate Component Signature

frontmatter.description: Generates component interface and props definition

tasks.instructions: Generate a React component signature with proper TypeScript interface for props. Include all necessary imports from React and any UI libraries. The interface should be descriptive with proper types and optional flags where appropriate.

tasks.name: Generate Component Structure

frontmatter.description: Generates the basic component structure and state

tasks.instructions: Generate the component's internal structure including useState hooks for managing component state and useEffect hooks for side effects. Each hook should have a clear purpose and proper dependency arrays.

tasks.name: Generate JSX Structure
tasks.instructions: Generate clean, semantic JSX markup using the project's CSS framework (Tailwind CSS). Include proper accessibility attributes (ARIA labels, roles), semantic HTML elements, and responsive design considerations.

tasks.name: Generate Event Handlers

frontmatter.description: Generates event handlers and helper functions

tasks.instructions: Generate event handlers and helper functions needed by the component. Each handler should be properly typed, handle errors appropriately, and follow React best practices (useCallback where beneficial). Include proper type guards and validation.

tasks.name: Generate Tests

frontmatter.description: Generates component tests with Testing Library

tasks.instructions: Generate comprehensive tests using React Testing Library and Vitest. Include tests for: rendering with different props, user interactions (click, type, submit), accessibility attributes, and edge cases. Use getBy*, queryBy*, and findBy* selectors appropriately. Include setup helpers and mock data where needed.

flow_api_integration.description: Creates a complete API integration layer with typed clients and error handling

tasks.name: Generate API Types

frontmatter.description: Generates TypeScript types for API requests/responses

tasks.instructions: Generate TypeScript interfaces and types for API requests, responses, and error handling. Include proper type discrimination for union types, optional fields marked correctly, and proper nested object types. Consider pagination, filtering, and sorting types if applicable.

tasks.name: Generate API Client

frontmatter.description: Generates the base API client with fetch/axios

tasks.instructions: Generate a configured API client using fetch or axios with base URL configuration, timeout handling, and request/response interceptors for auth tokens and error handling. Include proper TypeScript typing for the client instance.

tasks.name: Generate API Endpoints
tasks.instructions: Generate typed API endpoint functions for CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete, List). Each function should have proper parameter typing, return type promises, and error handling. Include query parameter support and request body typing where applicable.

tasks.name: Generate Error Handling

frontmatter.description: Generates error handling and retry logic

tasks.instructions: Generate error handling classes and utilities including custom API error types, error response parsing, retry logic with exponential backoff, and request cancellation support. Handle network errors, timeout errors, and HTTP error status codes appropriately.

tasks.name: Generate Data Hooks

frontmatter.description: Generates React hooks for data fetching

tasks.instructions: Generate custom React hooks that wrap the API endpoints for common data fetching patterns. Include hooks for: fetching single items, fetching lists with pagination, mutations (create/update/delete), and optimistic updates. Return consistent loading, error, and data states.

tasks.name: Generate API Tests
tasks.instructions: Generate tests for the API integration layer using MSW for mocking. Include tests for: successful requests, error responses, loading states, retry logic, and request cancellation. Set up proper request/response handlers for mocked API calls.

flow_hook_generate.description: Creates a complete custom React hook with proper typing and tests

tasks.name: Generate Hook Signature
tasks.instructions: Generate a custom React hook signature with properly typed parameters and return value. The hook name should start with 'use'. Include clear TypeScript types for all parameters and the return type (typically an array or object for multiple return values).

tasks.name: Generate Hook Logic

frontmatter.description: Generates hook implementation with state and effects

tasks.instructions: Generate the hook's internal logic including state management with useState and side effects with useEffect. Include proper cleanup functions in effects, correct dependency arrays, and memoization with useMemo/useCallback where appropriate for performance.

tasks.name: Generate Hook Utilities

frontmatter.description: Generates helper functions and error handling

tasks.instructions: Generate any helper functions and error handling logic needed by the hook. Include try-catch blocks for async operations, proper error types, and error recovery strategies. Helpers should be pure functions where possible and properly typed.

tasks.name: Generate Hook Tests

frontmatter.description: Generates tests for the custom hook

tasks.instructions: Generate comprehensive tests for the custom hook using @testing-library/react-hooks. Include tests for: initial state, state updates, effect execution, cleanup, error cases, and edge cases. Use act() for state updates and waitFor for async operations.

config.label: Feature Requirements
config.description: Specific feature requirements and functionality to implement

children.content:
# Custom Hook

A reusable React hook.

**Consider:**
- Hook signature and return type
- Effect cleanup
- Dependency arrays
- Error handling
- TypeScript typing

**Output:**
- Hook implementation
- TypeScript types
- Tests with @testing-library/react-hooks
- Usage documentation

# Form / Data Entry

A form with validation and submission.

**Consider:**
- Form library (react-hook-form, formik)
- Validation rules and schema
- Error display
- Success/error states
- Loading states
- Accessibility (labels, errors)

**Output:**
- Form component
- Validation schema
- Submit handler
- Error handling

# Authentication

User authentication and authorization.

**Consider:**
- Auth provider (Auth0, Clerk, Supabase)
- Session management
- Protected routes
- Token refresh
- Logout flow
- Role-based access

**Security:**
- Secure token storage
- HTTPS only
- CSRF protection
- Proper error handling (no info leakage)

# UI Component

A reusable UI component.

**Consider:**
- Props interface and defaults
- Accessibility attributes
- Responsive behavior
- Loading/error states
- Variant styles
- Test coverage

**Output:**
- Component file
- Props TypeScript interface
- Storybook stories
- Unit tests

config.label: Developer Profile
config.description: Developer experience level, specialization, and coding preferences

children.content:
# Frontend Development

Focus on UI/UX implementation and user interactions.

**Emphasize:**
- Component design and composition
- Accessibility (ARIA, keyboard navigation)
- Responsive layouts
- Browser compatibility
- Performance (lazy loading, code splitting)
- CSS best practices

# Backend Development

Focus on server-side logic and data management.

**Emphasize:**
- API design and documentation
- Database optimization
- Authentication/authorization
- Caching strategies
- Error handling
- Rate limiting and security

# Accessibility (a11y)

Focus on inclusive design for all users.

**Emphasize:**
- Semantic HTML elements
- ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management
- Color contrast requirements
- Alternative text for images

# Performance Optimization

Focus on making applications fast and efficient.

**Emphasize:**
- Bundle size optimization
- Code splitting strategies
- Memoization techniques
- Lazy loading patterns
- Network optimization
- Rendering performance
- Profiling and measurement

# Full Stack Development

Comfortable across the entire application stack.

**Emphasize:**
- Integration patterns between frontend/backend
- Data flow and state synchronization
- End-to-end features
- Deployment considerations
- Monitoring and debugging
- API contracts and types

# Type-Safe (TypeScript)

Leverage TypeScript's type system for safer code.

**Patterns:**
- Strict mode enabled
- Interface for public APIs
- Type aliases for unions
- Generics for reusable code
- Type guards for narrowing
- No `any` without justification

**Benefits:**
- Catch errors at compile time
- Better IDE support
- Self-documenting code

# Test-Driven Development

Write tests before implementation.

**Workflow:**
1. Write failing test
2. Write minimal code to pass
3. Refactor
4. Repeat

**Benefits:**
- Confirmed requirements
- Safer refactoring
- Living documentation
- Fewer bugs

# Functional Programming

Emphasize immutable data and pure functions.

**Patterns:**
- Avoid mutations, use spread operators
- Pure functions without side effects
- Map/filter/reduce over loops
- Function composition
- Higher-order functions

**Benefits:**
- Predictable code
- Easier testing
- Better debugging

config.label: Experience Level
config.description: Developer's experience level in web development

children.content:
# Intermediate (2-5 years)

Comfortable with core concepts, learning advanced patterns.

**Provide:**
- Efficient, idiomatic solutions
- Introduce common patterns
- Explain trade-offs of approaches
- Best practices with reasoning
- Performance considerations

**Focus Areas:**
- Design patterns
- Performance optimization
- Testing strategies
- State management patterns

# Advanced (5-10 years)

Deep understanding, focusing on optimization and architecture.

**Provide:**
- Advanced pattern implementations
- Performance optimization techniques
- Architecture discussions
- Edge case handling
- Scalability considerations

**Assumes:**
- Strong understanding of fundamentals
- Familiarity with common patterns
- Ability to reason about trade-offs

**Focus on:**
- Micro-optimizations
- Advanced TypeScript patterns
- Complex state scenarios
- Architecture decisions

# Beginner (0-2 years)

Early in the development journey. Focus on learning fundamentals.

**Provide:**
- Detailed explanations of concepts
- Comments explaining why, not just what
- Links to documentation for learning
- Simple, straightforward solutions
- Avoid complex patterns until understood

**Avoid:**
- Advanced patterns without explanation
- Assuming prior knowledge
- Skipping over fundamentals

config.label: Architecture & Patterns
config.description: Architectural patterns, design principles, and best practices

children.content:
# Compound Components

Parent and child components share implicit state.

**Example:**
```tsx
<Tabs>
  <Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Tab>One</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab>Two</Tabs.Tab>
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panels>
    <Tabs.Panel>Content 1</Tabs.Panel>
    <Tabs.Panel>Content 2</Tabs.Panel>
  </Tabs.Panels>
</Tabs>
```

**Implementation:**
- Context for shared state
- Compound components use context
- Flexible composition for users

# Observer / Pub-Sub

Components react to state changes automatically.

**In React:**
- Use for global state
- Components subscribe to changes
- Re-render on state updates

**Implementation:**
- Redux, Zustand, RxJS
- Custom hooks with context
- Event emitters

# Custom Hooks Pattern

Extract reusable stateful logic.

**Naming:**
- Always start with `use`
- Reflect the purpose: `useFetch`, `useForm`

**When to Create:**
- Duplicated stateful logic
- Complex state with side effects
- Shared behavior across components

**Example:**
```tsx
function useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initial: T) {
  // ... implementation
}
```

# Component-Based Architecture

Build UI as composable, reusable components.

**Principles:**
- Single responsibility per component
- Props in, events out
- Composition over inheritance
- Container vs presentational components

**File Structure:**
```
Component/
  Component.tsx
  Component.test.tsx
  Component.stories.tsx
  index.ts
```

Keep components small and focused.

# Micro-Frontends

Decompose frontend into independently deployable units.

**Patterns:**
- Module Federation
- Single-SPA
- Frame routing
- Web Components for sharing

**Considerations:**
- Shared dependency management
- Cross-app communication
- Consistent styling
- Performance overhead

config.label: Project Context
config.description: Project type, tech stack, and development environment configuration

children.content:
# React Context

Built-in state sharing across components.

**Best Practices:**
- Split contexts by domain
- Use separate contexts for state and dispatch
- Memoize context values to prevent re-renders
- Consider lifting state before context

Avoid context for frequently changing values (bubbles re-renders).

# Redux Toolkit

Predictable state container with best practices built-in.

**Pattern:**
- Slices for domain state
- Async thunks for API calls
- Selectors for derived data
- Immer for immutable updates

Use RTK Query for server state caching instead of manual async handling.

# Zustand

Small, fast state management solution.

**Usage:**
```tsx
const useStore = create((set) => ({
  count: 0,
  increment: () => set((state) => ({ count: state.count + 1 }))
}))
```

**Benefits:**
- Minimal boilerplate
- No providers needed
- TypeScript support
- Can be used outside React
- DevTools integration

# Styled Components

CSS-in-JS library with styled components and theme support.

**Usage:**
```tsx
const Button = styled.button<ButtonProps>`
  background: ${props => props.primary ? 'blue' : 'gray'};
  padding: 12px 24px;
`
```

**Features:**
- Dynamic styling via props
- Theme provider
- Automatic vendor prefixing
- No class name conflicts

# CSS Modules

Scoped CSS by generating unique class names.

**Usage:**
```tsx
import styles from './Component.module.css'

<div className={styles.container} />
```

**Benefits:**
- No global namespace pollution
- Dead code elimination
- Composable styles
- Type-safe with CSS modules typedef

# Tailwind CSS

Utility-first CSS framework for rapid UI development.

**Usage:**
- Use utility classes for styling
- `@apply` for component classes
- `theme()` function for design tokens
- Responsive prefixes: `sm:`, `md:`, `lg:`, `xl:`
- State variants: `hover:`, `focus:`, `active:`

Enable `jit` mode for smaller CSS bundles.

config.label: Project Type
config.description: The type of web project being developed

children.content:
# Single Page Application (SPA)

This is a Single Page Application built with modern JavaScript frameworks. SPAs load a single HTML page and dynamically update content as the user interacts with the app.

**Characteristics:**
- Client-side routing
- Dynamic content loading
- Smooth transitions without page refresh
- API-driven data fetching

Consider bundle size optimization and initial load performance.

# Server-Side Rendered (SSR)

This application uses Server-Side Rendering to generate HTML on the server for each request.

**Characteristics:**
- Faster initial page load
- Better SEO capabilities
- Server can access data during render
- May require Node.js server

Consider server load and caching strategies.

# Progressive Web App (PWA)

This is a Progressive Web App that combines web and native app features.

**Characteristics:**
- Installable on home screen
- Works offline with service workers
- Push notification support
- App-like experience

Implement service worker caching strategies and app manifests.

# React

Component-based UI library using virtual DOM for efficient rendering.

**Key Patterns:**
- Functional components with hooks
- Props for data flow
- Context for state sharing
- Controlled components for forms

Use `useCallback`, `useMemo` for performance optimization.

# Next.js

React framework with SSR, SSG, and API routes.

**Key Features:**
- File-based routing
- Server components (RSC)
- API routes for backend
- Built-in image optimization
- App router with layouts

Use server components for static content, client for interactivity.

# Svelte

Compile-time framework that generates vanilla JavaScript.

**Key Characteristics:**
- No virtual DOM overhead
- Built-in state management
- Transitions and animations
- Smaller bundle sizes

Reactivity is built into assignments. No need for special state APIs.

# Vue.js

Progressive framework with reactive data binding.

**Key Patterns:**
- Template syntax with directives
- Reactive refs and computed
- Composition API
- Single File Components (.vue)

Use `computed` for derived state, `watch` for side effects.

# Testing Library

User-centric testing approach.

**Principles:**
- Test user behavior, not implementation
- Queries mirror how users find elements
- Avoid testing internals

**Preferred queries (in order):**
1. `getByRole` - most accessible
2. `getByLabelText` - form elements
3. `getByPlaceholderText` - inputs
4. `getByText` - content

Avoid `getByTestId` unless necessary.

# Vitest

Fast unit testing with native ESM support.

**Features:**
- Jest-compatible API
- Out-of-box TypeScript/JSX
- Watch mode with HMR
- Built-in code coverage

Use `describe`, `it`, `expect` as in Jest. Imports work natively without transforms.

# Playwright

End-to-end testing for modern web apps.

**Features:**
- Cross-browser testing
- Fast parallel execution
- Auto-waiting for elements
- Network interception
- Visual regression

Write tests that verify critical user flows: login, checkout, form submissions.

frontmatter.name: LessonPlanAgent
frontmatter.description: Creates comprehensive lesson plans aligned with standards, customized for classroom and teacher needs

agent-lesson-plan.mainInstruction:
# Lesson Plan Assistant

You are an expert instructional coach helping teachers create engaging, standards-aligned lesson plans. 

## Your Approach
- Always start by understanding the specific classroom context and student needs
- Suggest active learning strategies that promote student engagement
- Include differentiation for diverse learners
- Provide clear learning objectives and success criteria
- Align all activities to the selected curriculum standards

## Output Format
Structure your lesson plans with clear sections including objectives, materials, procedure, assessment, and differentiation.


<slash_action name="Generate Lesson" description="Create a complete lesson plan with structured sections" flowId="flow_lesson_generate">
/generate
</slash_action>

slashActions.name: Generate Lesson
slashActions.description: Create a complete lesson plan with structured sections

messages.content: I need a lesson plan for introducing fractions to my 5th graders. Many of them are ELL students.

I'll create a fractions lesson plan designed for your 5th grade class with ELL students. Let me align it to Common Core standards.

## Lesson: Understanding Fractions as Numbers

**Standards:** 5.NF.A.1 - Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators

**Learning Objectives:**
- Students will understand fractions as numbers on the number line
- Students will visualize fractions using area models
- Students will compare fractions with unlike denominators

**Essential Question:** How can we represent parts of a whole and compare them?

**Differentiation for ELL:**
- Visual supports with fraction tiles and manipulatives
- Vocabulary bank with pictures (numerator, denominator, equivalent)
- Sentence frames for mathematical discourse
- Partner work with mixed-language pairing

Would you like me to expand any section or add specific activities?
/generate --unitFocus "Introduction to Fractions" --lessonLength 60 --essentialQuestions "How do fractions represent parts of a whole?"

parameters.essentialQuestions: How do fractions represent parts of a whole?

messages.content: Generating lesson plan structure...

frontmatter.name: AssessmentAgent
frontmatter.description: Designs formative and summative assessments aligned to learning objectives and standards

agent-assessment.mainInstruction:
<slash_action name="Generate Assessment" description="Create a complete assessment with structured sections" flowId="flow_assessment_generate">
/generate
</slash_action>

slashActions.name: Generate Assessment
slashActions.description: Create a complete assessment with structured sections

formValues.essentialQuestions: How can we represent real-world situations with equations?

messages.content: Create a quiz on linear equations for my 8th graders. Include accommodations for students with IEPs.

I'll create a linear equations quiz with appropriate accommodations.

## Linear Equations Quiz (8th Grade)

**Standards:** 8.EE.C.7 - Solve linear equations in one variable

### Questions (10 points total)

1. Solve: 3x + 5 = 20 (2 pts)
2. Solve: 2(x - 4) = 18 (2 pts)
3. Write an equation for: Five more than twice a number is 23 (2 pts)
4. Solve and graph: x - 7 ≤ -3 (2 pts)
5. Word problem: A phone plan costs $15 plus $0.10 per minute. Write an equation and find cost for 100 minutes. (2 pts)

### Accommodations for IEP/504:
- **Reduced items**: Provide 5 key questions instead of 10
- **Calculator**: Allow for all computation
- **Formula sheet**: Provide equation-solving steps reference
- **Extended time**: 1.5x time allowance
- **Read aloud**: Test items read to student
- **Scribe**: Student responds orally, scribe records

### Modified Version (Alternative Assessment):
For students requiring significant modification, consider a performance task where students demonstrate solving one multi-step equation with verbal explanation of each step.

Would you like a rubric for the word problem or additional modifications?
/generate --assessmentType Quiz --learningObjectives "Solve linear equations in one variable" --pointValue 100

parameters.learningObjectives: Solve linear equations in one variable

messages.content: Generating assessment structure...

rubric.fullCredit: Correct answer (x = 13) with complete work shown
rubric.partialCredit: Correct answer with partial work OR correct process with calculation error

items.question: Write an equation for: Five more than twice a number is 23. Then solve it.

rubric.partialCredit: Correct equation or correct solution only

flow_lesson_generate.description: LessonPlanAgent flow: Creates a complete lesson plan with 8 gradual section generation tasks

tasks.name: Generate Title and Overview

frontmatter.description: Generates lesson plan title and overview section

tasks.instructions: Generate an engaging, standards-aligned lesson title and comprehensive overview. The title should be descriptive and student-friendly. Include grade level, subject area, estimated duration, and a concise overview that summarizes the lesson's focus, key activities, and learning goals. The overview should give a teacher a clear understanding of what the lesson entails at a glance.

tasks.name: Generate Objectives and Standards

frontmatter.description: Generates learning objectives and aligned standards

tasks.instructions: Generate specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) learning objectives written in student-friendly language. Include aligned curriculum standards with identifiers (e.g., CCSS, state standards). Craft an essential question that frames the lesson's core inquiry and drives student curiosity. Define clear success criteria that help students and teachers assess whether learning goals have been met.

tasks.name: Generate Materials and Preparation

frontmatter.description: Generates materials list and preparation instructions

tasks.instructions: List all materials needed for the lesson, including consumables, manipulatives, technology, and printed resources. Include quantities where relevant. Provide step-by-step preparation instructions that a teacher should complete before the lesson begins. Suggest supplemental resources such as books, websites, videos, or reference materials that enhance the lesson.

tasks.name: Generate Opening Section
tasks.instructions: Design an engaging opening (5-10 minutes) that activates prior knowledge, builds curiosity, and connects to students' experiences. Include a hook activity such as a thought-provoking question, quick hands-on exploration, visual stimulus, or real-world connection. Provide clear teacher instructions for facilitating the opening and transitioning smoothly into the main lesson content.

tasks.name: Generate Instruction Section
tasks.instructions: Create a structured direct instruction sequence that explicitly teaches the core content. Break down complex concepts into clear, scaffolded steps. Include teacher think-aloud scripts that model cognitive processes. Incorporate checks for understanding, visual supports, and examples/non-examples. Ensure the progression builds logically and addresses common misconceptions.

tasks.name: Generate Practice Sections

frontmatter.description: Generates guided and independent practice sections

tasks.instructions: Design both guided and independent practice activities. Guided practice should include collaborative learning with teacher support, suggested groupings (pairs, small groups), and structured activities that apply the newly learned content. Independent practice should provide individual application opportunities with clear task descriptions and expected products. Ensure a gradual release of responsibility from guided to independent work.

tasks.name: Generate Closure and Assessment

frontmatter.description: Generates lesson closure and assessment sections

tasks.instructions: Create a meaningful closure that synthesizes learning and connects to future lessons. Include exit ticket activities, summarization strategies, or reflective discussions. Provide formative assessment strategies for checking understanding during the lesson and describe any summative assessment that follows. Closure should reinforce key concepts and celebrate learning progress.

tasks.name: Generate Differentiation Strategies

frontmatter.description: Generates differentiation strategies for diverse learners

tasks.instructions: Provide specific differentiation strategies for diverse learners including English Language Learners, students with IEPs/504 plans, advanced learners, and struggling students. Include content modifications, process adjustments, product alternatives, and environmental considerations. Suggest extension activities for students who need additional challenge. Ensure all students can access and engage with the lesson content.

flow_assessment_generate.description: AssessmentAgent flow: Creates a complete assessment with 6 gradual section generation tasks

tasks.name: Generate Assessment Overview

frontmatter.description: Generates assessment title and overview section

tasks.instructions: Generate a comprehensive assessment overview including a clear, descriptive title; assessment type (quiz, test, performance task, portfolio, etc.); grade level and subject area; estimated duration; and a brief overview explaining what knowledge and skills are being assessed. The overview should help teachers quickly understand the assessment's purpose and scope.

tasks.name: Generate Assessment Items Section
tasks.instructions: Create varied assessment items that measure student understanding at different Depth of Knowledge levels. Include multiple choice, short answer, and extended response items. Each item should have a clear, unambiguous question; appropriate point values; alignment to specific standards; and designated DOK level. Questions should progress from recall to higher-order thinking.

itemType.description: Multiple choice, short answer, essay, etc.

tasks.name: Generate Answer Key
tasks.instructions: Generate a comprehensive answer key with correct answers for all objective items. Include scoring guides for open-ended responses that specify point values and performance criteria for each score level. Provide clear guidance on partial credit allocation and common misconceptions that may affect scoring.

additionalProperties.description: Correct answer for each item number

tasks.name: Generate Rubric Section
tasks.instructions: Create a clear, criterion-referenced scoring rubric with performance indicators for each level (Exemplary, Proficient, Developing, Beginning). Each criterion should have specific descriptors that distinguish between performance levels. Include point values for each criterion. The rubric should be user-friendly for both teachers and students.

tasks.name: Generate Accommodations Section
tasks.instructions: List appropriate accommodations for diverse learner groups including English Language Learners, students with IEPs, 504 plans, and reading difficulties. Include timing accommodations, presentation modifications, response formats, and environmental considerations. Ensure accommodations maintain assessment validity while providing equitable access.

tasks.name: Generate Administration Guide
tasks.instructions: Create a comprehensive administration guide including preparation steps (materials needed, room setup, advance notices); total time allocation; and suggested timing breakdowns by section. Include instructions for test security, student communication, and handling special circumstances during administration.

config.label: Teacher Profile
config.description: Teacher experience, specialization, and teaching preferences

children.content:
# 3-5 Years Experience

## Profile
- Finding groove, developing confidence
- Establishing consistent routines and approaches
- Beginning to differentiate and vary instruction

## Strengths
- Beginning to understand student development
- Classroom management becoming more automatic
- Can plan more efficiently

## Growth Areas
- Deepening content knowledge
- Data-driven instruction
- Special populations expertise

# 6-10 Years Experience

## Profile
- Solid foundation of management and instruction
- Planning efficiently, managing multiple demands
- Can analyze and adjust based on results

## Strengths
- Strong classroom management
- Deep understanding of curriculum
- Effective parent communication

## Growth Areas
- Avoiding stagnation
- Mentoring newer teachers
- Leadership opportunities

# 11-20 Years Experience

## Profile
- Extensive experience across contexts
- Highly effective with most students
- Often in leadership or mentoring roles

## Strengths
- Deep understanding of development
- Viable strategies for most challenges
- Efficient systems and routines

## Growth Areas
- Avoiding cynicism or burnout
- Staying current with trends
- Work-life balance

# 20+ Years Experience

## Profile
- Career-long dedication to teaching
- Seen trends come and go
- Valued for historical perspective and expertise

## Strengths
- Incredible depth of experience
- Master classroom manager
- Wealth of strategies

## Considerations
- Risk of becoming set in ways
- Stay curious and open to innovation
- Mentor while being open to learning from newcomers

frontmatter.title: 0-2 years (Novice)
frontmatter.description: Early career teacher building foundational skills

children.content:
# 0-2 Years Experience (Novice)

## Profile
- Building foundations, steep learning curve
- Energy and enthusiasm for new ideas
- Benefits from mentorship and structured guidance

## Strengths
- Open to new approaches
- Current on educational theory
- Technologically adept

## Growth Areas
- Classroom management consistency
- Pacing and planning efficiency
- Parent communication confidence

## Advice
- Focus on a few key practices
- Seek mentorship actively

# Reading Specialist Certification

- **Focus:** Advanced expertise in literacy instruction
- **Role:** Reading intervention, literacy coaching, RtI coordination
- **Expertise:** Reading acquisition, assessment, interventions, dyslexia

Works with struggling readers and supports other teachers

# ESL/ELL Endorsement

- **All Grades:** Typically K-12
- **Focus:** Second language acquisition, sheltered instruction
- **Expertise:** Working with culturally and linguistically diverse students

May pull out students or co-teach with general education

# General Elementary Certification

- **Grades:** Typically K-6 or K-8
- **All Subjects:** Qualified to teach all core subjects
- **Focus:** Child development and learning, cross-curricular integration

- Generalist rather than specialist
- Strong understanding of learning progressions

# Special Education Certification

- **All Grades:** K-12 or grade-band specific
- **Focus:** Specialized instruction, IEP development and implementation
- **Expertise:** Behavior management, collaboration, case management

Critical shortage area in many districts

# Gifted Education Certification

- **All Grades:** Typically K-12
- **Focus:** Advanced learners, acceleration and enrichment strategies
- **Expertise:** Social-emotional needs of gifted students, identification

Often combined with other responsibilities

# Subject-Specific Secondary Certification

- **Grades:** Typically 7-12 or 5-12
- **Subject Area:** Certified in specific content area(s)
- **Focus:** Adolescent development, secondary methods

Common areas: Math, English, Sciences, Social Studies, Foreign Languages, Art, Music, PE

config.label: Subject Expertise
config.hint: Select all areas of certification or expertise

children.content:
# Social Studies Expertise

## Content Areas
- **History:** Events, figures, periods, cause and effect
- **Geography:** Maps, places, regions, human-environment interaction
- **Civics:** Citizenship, democracy, laws, institutions
- **Economics:** Resources, production, consumption, trade

## Pedagogy
- Inquiry-based learning
- Primary source analysis
- Multiple perspectives and historiography

# Special Education Expertise

## Knowledge Areas
- **Disabilities:** Specific learning disabilities, autism, emotional/behavioral
- **Legal:** IDEA, FAPE, LRE, due process
- **IEP Process:** Referral, evaluation, planning, implementation
- **Instruction:** Specially designed instruction

## Pedagogy
- Accommodations vs. modifications
- Universal Design for Learning
- Positive behavior support
- Collaboration with general education

# Physical Education Expertise

## Content Areas
- **Motor Skills:** Locomotor, non-locomotor, manipulative
- **Fitness:** Cardiovascular, strength, endurance, flexibility
- **Games & Sports:** Skills, strategies, rules, sportsmanship
- **Anatomy:** Body systems, exercise science

## Pedagogy
- Age-appropriate skill progression
- Modification for all ability levels
- Lifetime fitness focus

# Music Expertise

## Content Areas
- **Elements:** Rhythm, melody, harmony, form, timbre, expression
- **Theory:** Notation, scales, intervals, chords, composition
- **History:** Periods, composers, styles, cultural traditions
- **Performance:** Vocal and instrumental techniques

## Pedagogy
- Active music-making (singing, playing, creating)
- Music literacy development
- Cultural and historical contexts

# Mathematics Expertise

## Content Areas
- **Number Sense:** Place value, operations, fractions, decimals
- **Algebra:** Patterns, equations, functions, proportional reasoning
- **Geometry:** Shapes, transformations, measurement, spatial reasoning
- **Statistics:** Data analysis, graphing, probability

## Pedagogy
- Concrete-Representational-Abstract progression
- Multiple representations (visual, symbolic, verbal, contextual)
- Focus on conceptual understanding before procedures

# ELA Expertise

## Content Areas
- **Reading:** Foundational skills, fluency, comprehension strategies
- **Writing:** Process writing, genres, grammar, conventions
- **Speaking & Listening:** Discussion skills, presentations
- **Language:** Vocabulary, conventions, etymology

## Pedagogy
- Balanced literacy approach
- Reader's and writer's workshop models
- Integration of reading and writing

# Art Expertise

## Content Areas
- **Elements:** Line, shape, color, texture, form, space, value
- **Principles:** Balance, contrast, emphasis, movement, pattern, unity
- **Media:** Drawing, painting, sculpture, printmaking, digital
- **Art History:** Styles, periods, artists, cultural contexts

## Pedagogy
- Creative expression and experimentation
- Art appreciation and visual literacy
- Studio processes management

# Science Expertise

## Content Areas
- **Physical Science:** Matter, energy, forces, motion
- **Life Science:** Organisms, ecosystems, heredity, evolution
- **Earth & Space:** Geology, weather, astronomy, water cycle
- **Engineering Design:** Problem-solving, iteration

## Pedagogy
- Scientific inquiry and investigation
- Hands-on experimentation
- Science and engineering practices

# ESL/ELL Expertise

## Knowledge Areas
- **Acquisition:** Theories, stages, factors affecting language learning
- **Proficiency:** WIDA levels, entering through bridging
- **Linguistics:** Phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics
- **Programs:** Bilingual, sheltered, dual language models

## Pedagogy
- Sheltered instruction (SIOP, GLAD)
- Content and language objectives
- Building on home language assets

config.label: Professional Development Goals
config.placeholder: Describe areas for growth or improvement...

children.content:
# Project-Based Learning

Extended projects with authentic real-world connections.

## Best For
- Integrating multiple subjects
- Deeper understanding and application
- Student engagement and relevance

## Key Elements
- Driving question or challenge
- Sustained inquiry
- Authentic context and audience
- Student decisions and reflection
- Public product

# Cooperative Learning

Structured group work with individual accountability.

## Best For
- Practice and review activities
- Complex problem-solving
- Social skill development
- Diverse perspectives

## Key Elements
- Clear group goals and individual roles
- Face-to-face interaction
- Interpersonal skills taught explicitly
- Group processing/reflection

# Gamification

Game design elements to increase motivation.

## Best For
- Skill practice and repetition
- Building engagement
- Progress tracking
- Positive class culture

## Key Elements
- Clear goals and rules
- Progress tracking and feedback
- Rewards and recognition
- Narrative or context
- Balanced challenge

# Direct Instruction

Teacher-led, explicit teaching with clear modeling.

## Best For
- Introducing new skills or content
- Teaching procedures and routines
- Ensuring all students access core content
- Clarifying misconceptions

## Key Elements
- Clear learning objectives
- Modeling and think-alouds
- Guided practice with feedback
- Independent practice

# Inquiry-Based Learning

Student-led investigation and discovery.

## Best For
- Science and social studies investigations
- Developing critical thinking
- Building curiosity and engagement
- Memorable learning experiences

## Key Elements
- Compelling questions
- Investigation and research
- Evidence-based conclusions
- Communication of findings

# Differentiated Instruction

Responsive teaching adjusted to student needs.

## What's Differentiated
- **Content:** What students learn
- **Process:** How students learn
- **Product:** How students demonstrate learning
- **Environment:** Conditions for learning

## Best For
- Diverse classrooms with varied readiness
- Multiple learning profiles
- Ensuring access and engagement for all

config.label: Classroom Management
config.description: Classroom characteristics, student demographics, and learning environment configuration

config.label: Available Resources
config.description: Physical and digital resources available in the classroom
config.hint: Select all resources that are available

frontmatter.title: Library Access
frontmatter.description: School library available for research and reading

children.content:
# Library Access

## Uses
- Research projects and inquiry learning
- Book selection and independent reading
- Digital literacy and database skills
- Literature circles and book clubs

## Benefits
- Diverse fiction and nonfiction titles
- Librarian support for research
- Quiet reading and study space

# Interactive Whiteboard

## Uses
- Modeling think-alouds and problem-solving
- Virtual manipulatives and tools
- Student demonstrations and sharing
- Interactive games and activities
- Annotation of texts and diagrams

## Keep Active
- Avoid passive viewing
- Have students interact with the board
- Always backup plan for technical issues

# Gym/Outdoor Space Access

## Spaces
- Gymnasium: Large indoor space
- Playground/Field: Outdoor active play
- Track/Trail: Walking or running paths

## Uses
- Physical education and motor skills
- Brain breaks and movement
- Outdoor learning and exploration
- Science observations (nature, weather)
- Team building and cooperative games

frontmatter.title: 1:1 Devices
frontmatter.description: Each student has their own device

children.content:
# 1:1 Device Program

## Uses
- Adaptive learning software (IXL, Khan Academy)
- Research and information literacy
- Digital creation (slides, videos, documents)
- Formative assessment tools (Kahoot, Quizizz)
- Collaborative platforms (shared docs)

## Considerations
- Digital citizenship education
- Screen time balance
- Device management and charging

# Art Supplies

## Materials
- Drawing: Paper, pencils, markers, crayons
- Painting: Watercolors, tempera, brushes
- Construction: Glue, scissors, construction paper
- Modeling: Clay, playdough

## Uses
- Creative expression and artistic skill development
- Illustrating understanding of content
- Cross-curricular projects
- Fine motor skill development

frontmatter.title: Music Room
frontmatter.description: Access to music facilities and instruments

children.content:
# Music Room Access

## Resources
- Instruments: Percussion, keyboards, recorders
- Large open space for movement
- Audio equipment and recording capability
- Music teacher collaboration

## Uses
- Music instruction and skill development
- Cultural exploration through music
- Performance preparation
- Integration with other subjects

# Science Lab Access

## Uses
- Hands-on experiments and investigations
- Inquiry-based science learning
- Data collection and analysis
- Lab skills development

## Considerations
- Safety procedures and training essential
- Advance preparation and setup required
- Supervision ratios for safe lab work

config.label: Class Size
config.description: Number of students in the class

children.content:
# Large Class Size (26-35)

## Challenges
- Limited individual attention time
- Assessment monitoring difficult

## Best Strategies
- Structured routines and procedures
- Cooperative learning with roles
- Peer tutoring
- Technology-assisted practice

# Medium Class Size (16-25)

## Characteristics
- Standard school model
- Balanced individual/group instruction
- Manageable for teacher to know each student

## Best Strategies
- Small group rotations
- Partner and collaborative structures
- Flexible grouping

# Very Large Class Size (36+)

## Significant Challenges
- Management is primary concern
- Individual instruction highly constrained

## Essential Strategies
- Highly structured routines (every minute planned)
- Station teaching rotations
- Peer assessment structures
- Para-educator support if available

# Small Class Size (1-15)

## Benefits
- Individualized attention daily
- Flexible pacing based on needs
- Strong relationships
- Efficient formative assessment

## Best Strategies
- Socratic seminars
- Individual conferencing
- Personalized projects

config.label: Grade Level
config.description: The grade level of the class

children.content:
# 4th Grade

**Ages:** 9-10 years

## Key Characteristics
- Multi-paragraph writing
- Complex problem-solving
- Attention span: 25-30 minutes

## Teaching Tips
- Research projects with multiple sources
- Collaborative work with defined roles
- Cross-curricular connections

# 12th Grade

**Ages:** 17-18 | **High School Senior**

## Key Characteristics
- Culminating high school experience
- College applications
- Transition planning

## Teaching Tips
- College essay support
- Senior capstone projects
- Transition planning (college/career)

# 9th Grade

**Ages:** 14-15 | **High School Freshman**

## Key Characteristics
- High school transition
- Credit-bearing courses begin
- GPA becomes significant

## Teaching Tips
- Orientation and success skills
- Note-taking strategies
- College and career exploration begins

# 10th Grade

**Ages:** 15-16 | **High School Sophomore**

## Key Characteristics
- AP/Honors options available
- Standardized test prep begins
- Identity exploration

## Teaching Tips
- PSAT preparation
- Research papers
- Career interest inventories

# 3rd Grade

**Ages:** 8-9 years

## Key Characteristics
- "Learning to read" shifts to "reading to learn"
- Multiplication and division introduced
- Attention span: 20-25 minutes

## Teaching Tips
- Content-area reading integration
- Project-based learning opportunities
- Introduction to test-taking strategies

# 1st Grade

**Ages:** 6-7 years

## Key Characteristics
- Transition to formal academics
- Beginning reading and writing development
- Attention span: 10-15 minutes

## Teaching Tips
- Balance direct instruction with hands-on activities
- Frequent checks for understanding
- Gradual increase in independent work

# 7th Grade

**Ages:** 12-13 | **Middle School Year 2**

## Key Characteristics
- Peak of early adolescence
- Heightened social awareness
- Abstract reasoning developing

## Teaching Tips
- Argumentative and analytical writing
- Scientific inquiry emphasis
- Technology integration

# 6th Grade

**Ages:** 11-12 | **Middle School Year 1**

## Key Characteristics
- Transition to middle school
- Multiple teachers and subjects
- Major social transitions

## Teaching Tips
- Support transition to new routines
- Organizational systems emphasis
- Social-emotional support

# 2nd Grade

**Ages:** 7-8 years

## Key Characteristics
- Strengthening reading fluency
- Beginning paragraph writing
- Attention span: 15-20 minutes

## Teaching Tips
- Guided reading and writing workshops
- Math manipulatives for concepts
- Collaborative learning structures

# 11th Grade

**Ages:** 16-17 | **High School Junior**

## Key Characteristics
- Most challenging academic year
- SAT/ACT preparation
- College considerations intensify

## Teaching Tips
- College search and planning
- Rigorous coursework support
- Junior year research projects

# Kindergarten (K)

**Ages:** 5-6 years

## Key Characteristics
- Learning through play and hands-on exploration
- Short attention span (5-10 minutes)
- Developing social skills: sharing, taking turns
- Fine motor skills developing

## Teaching Tips
- Short, engaging activities with movement
- Visual supports and hands-on materials
- Consistent routines essential

# 5th Grade

**Ages:** 10-11 years

## Key Characteristics
- Final year of elementary school
- Synthesis and critical thinking
- Attention span: 30+ minutes

## Teaching Tips
- Extended research and presentation projects
- Literature circles and book clubs
- Pre-algebraic concepts introduction

# 8th Grade

**Ages:** 13-14 | **Middle School Year 3**

## Key Characteristics
- Preparing for high school
- Algebra, literary analysis
- Leadership opportunities

## Teaching Tips
- High school preparation
- Capstone projects
- Self-advocacy skills

config.label: Student Support Needs
config.description: Special populations and support requirements in the class
config.hint: Select all that apply to your student population

frontmatter.title: English Language Learners
frontmatter.description: Students who are developing English proficiency

children.content:
# English Language Learners

## Proficiency Levels
Entering → Beginning → Developing → Expanding → Bridging

## Key Strategies
- **Visual Supports:** Images, gestures, graphic organizers, realia
- **Scaffolding:** Sentence frames, word banks, modeled language
- **Comprehensible Input:** Clear speech, written notes, avoid idioms
- **Wait Time:** Extra processing time for language formulation

## Assessment
- Separate content knowledge from English proficiency
- Allow multiple demonstration formats
- Extended time for assessments

# Math Intervention Students

## Profile
- Math skills below grade level
- Missing foundational concepts
- May have developed math anxiety

## Key Strategies
- **CRA Approach:** Concrete → Representational → Abstract
- **Explicit Instruction:** Clear modeling of thinking
- **Visual Representations:** Number lines, arrays, models
- **Fact Fluency:** Strategic practice of basics

## Accommodations
- Multiplication charts and reference sheets
- Calculator for concept understanding (not computation)
- Graphic organizers for problem-solving

frontmatter.title: Special Education
frontmatter.description: Students with IEPs or specialized learning plans

children.content:
# Special Education Students

## Legal Requirements
- IEP accommodations and modifications must be implemented
- Least Restrictive Environment (LRE)
- Individualized instruction per student needs

## Key Strategies
- **Universal Design for Learning:** Multiple means of representation, engagement, expression
- **Accommodations:** Changes in *how* students learn (not what)
- **Modifications:** Changes in *what* students learn (when specified)
- **Multi-sensory Instruction:** Visual, auditory, kinesthetic approaches

## Collaboration
- Consult with special education staff
- Review IEP goals and accommodations regularly

# Reading Intervention Students

## Profile
- Reading significantly below grade level
- May have phonics, fluency, or comprehension gaps

## Key Strategies
- **Structured Literacy:** Explicit, systematic phonics
- **Fluency Practice:** Repeated reading, modeled reading
- **Audio Support:** Text-to-speech for content access
- **Decodable Texts:** Materials matching phonics knowledge

## Accommodations
- Extended time for reading tasks
- Content read aloud for subject-area access
- Audiobooks for grade-level content

frontmatter.title: Behavioral Support
frontmatter.description: Students with behavioral or social-emotional needs

children.content:
# Behavioral Support Students

## Profile
- May have BIPs, 504 plans, or other support plans
- Behavior often reflects missing skills, not willful noncompliance
- Trauma-informed approach essential

## Key Strategies
- **Clear Expectations:** Explicitly teach desired behaviors
- **Predictable Routines:** Structure provides security
- **Positive Reinforcement:** Acknowledge desired behaviors
- **Breaks:** Scheduled movement or calm-down time
- **Choice:** Limited options for sense of control

## De-escalation
- Remain calm, use neutral tone
- Give space, avoid power struggles
- Private conversations rather than public corrections

# Gifted & Talented Students

## Characteristics
- Rapid learning with fewer repetitions
- Grasp abstract concepts readily
- Deep focus in areas of interest

## Key Strategies
- **Depth not Volume:** Complexity, novelty, not more work
- **Tiered Assignments:** Different complexity levels for same concept
- **Independent Study:** Pursuit of individual interests
- **Acceleration:** Subject or grade-level when appropriate

## Avoid
- Using gifted students as unpaid tutors
- Simply assigning more work
- Assuming they don't need support

# In-Person Learning

## Characteristics
- Face-to-face in physical classroom
- Immediate feedback and interaction
- Use of physical space and movement

## Best For
- Direct instruction with immediate checks
- Hands-on activities and manipulatives
- Rich social interactions
- Relationship building

frontmatter.title: Flipped Classroom
frontmatter.description: Content delivery at home, practice in class

children.content:
# Flipped Classroom

## Structure
- **Home:** Video lessons, readings, online explorations
- **Class:** Problem-solving, discussions, projects
- **Teacher Role:** Circulating, conferencing, clarifying

## Advantages
- Students can pause and rewind content
- Self-paced content delivery
- Teacher available when help is needed most

## Considerations
- Technology and internet access needed at home
- Accountability for at-home learning

frontmatter.title: Hybrid
frontmatter.description: Combination of in-person and remote learning

children.content:
# Hybrid Learning

## Characteristics
- Some students in-person, some remote (or alternating days)
- Synchronous + asynchronous elements
- Technology connects all learners

## Best For
- Concurrent teaching strategies
- Station rotation models
- Digital collaboration tools
- Flexible learning environments

## Challenge
- Teacher attention divided between settings

# Remote/Online Learning

## Characteristics
- All instruction occurs online
- Flexible location for students and teacher
- Technology-dependent

## Best For
- Asynchronous content delivery
- Synchronous sessions for community
- Digital collaboration platforms
- Office hours for individual support

## Challenges
- Technology access and support
- Screen time management
- Building community virtually

config.label: Subject Topics
config.description: Topic breakdowns and content areas by subject

children.content:
# Arts Integration

## Visual Arts
- Drawing to show understanding
- Diagrams and models for concepts
- Infographics combining text and visuals
- Color and emotion in literature/history

## Performing Arts
- Historical reenactment
- Acting out scientific processes
- Bringing texts to life
- Role-playing social situations

## Music
- Educational songs for memory
- Rhythm and mathematical patterns
- Cultural music for understanding

# Numeracy Integration

## Math in Science
- Data analysis: Collecting, representing, interpreting
- Measurement: Precision, units, error
- Proportional reasoning: Scaling, rates

## Math in Social Studies
- Data interpretation: Charts, graphs, statistics
- Timeline and chronology
- Geography: Coordinates, scale, distance
- Economics: Budgets, percentages

## Real-World Numeracy
- Financial literacy
- Measurement in daily life
- Statistics in media

# Social-Emotional Learning (CASEL Framework)

## Self-Awareness
Emotions, strengths/challenges, confidence, growth mindset

## Self-Management
Impulse control, stress management, self-discipline, motivation

## Social Awareness
Empathy, perspective-taking, appreciating diversity, respect

## Relationship Skills
Communication, teamwork, conflict resolution, seeking/helping

## Responsible Decision-Making
Ethical considerations, consequences, problem-solving, safety

## Integration Strategies
- Explicit teaching of SEL skills
- Morning meetings and class circles
- Literature with SEL themes
- Restorative practices

# Real-World Applications

## Community Connections
- Local issues: Environmental, social, civic problems
- Local history and primary sources
- Service learning projects
- Community experts and professionals

## Career Connections
- How subjects connect to professions
- Real-world professional problems
- Professional tools and methods

## Current Events
- News analysis using content concepts
- Real data from news and research
- Civic engagement opportunities

## Personal Relevance
- Financial literacy
- Health and wellness decisions
- Environmental stewardship

# Literacy Integration

## Reading Across Content Areas
- Content area reading strategies
- Disciplinary literacy (how experts read)
- Academic vocabulary development
- Text complexity access

## Writing Across the Curriculum
- Writing to learn: Journals, quick writes
- Argument writing: Claim-evidence-reasoning
- Informative/explanatory writing
- Writing frames and scaffolds

# Technology Integration

## Productivity Tools
- Word processing, spreadsheets, presentations
- Cloud storage and collaboration

## Subject-Specific Tools
- Science: Simulations, data sensors, digital microscopes
- Math: Graphing tools, geometry software
- Humanities: Digital archives, research databases

## Digital Citizenship
- Online safety and privacy
- Evaluating online information
- Responsible communication
- Copyright and ethical use

config.label: Topics to Cover
config.placeholder: Specific topics will be suggested based on subject

config.label: Essential Questions to Address
config.description: Big questions students should be able to answer
config.placeholder: What big questions should students be able to answer?

children.content:
# Social Studies

## Core Disciplines
- **History:** Historical thinking, context, evidence, arguments
- **Geography:** Spatial thinking, places, regions, physical/human systems
- **Civics & Government:** Civic life, government structure, constitutional principles, citizenship
- **Economics:** Scarcity, economic systems, markets, national economy, global economy

## Skills
- Critical thinking and analysis
- Primary and secondary source analysis
- Geographic literacy
- Civic participation

# Physical Education

## Motor Skills
- **Locomotor:** Walk, run, hop, gallop, skip, leap, jump
- **Non-Locomotor:** Bend, stretch, twist, balance
- **Manipulative:** Throw/catch, kick/punt, strike, dribble, volley

## Physical Fitness
- **Health-Related:** Cardiovascular, strength, endurance, flexibility, body composition
- **Skill-Related:** Agility, balance, coordination, power, reaction time, speed

## Social Responsibility
Goal setting, cooperation, teamwork, fair play, safety, etiquette

# Computer Science

## Computational Thinking
- Decomposition: Breaking problems into parts
- Pattern Recognition: Finding similarities
- Abstraction: Focusing on important information
- Algorithm Design: Step-by-step procedures

## Programming
- **Concepts:** Sequences, loops, events, conditionals, variables, functions
- **Block-Based:** Scratch, Blockly
- **Text-Based:** Python, JavaScript

## Digital Literacy & Citizenship
- Digital devices, internet and networks
- Online safety, evaluating information, ethical use, copyright

## Data and Applications
Robotics, game design, web development

# Music

## Musical Elements
- **Rhythm:** Beat, meter, tempo, patterns
- **Melody:** Pitch, contour, scales, intervals
- **Harmony:** Chords, accompaniment, texture
- **Form:** AB, ABA, rondo, theme/variation
- **Expression:** Dynamics, timbre, style

## Musical Skills
- Vocal: Posture, breath, pitch matching, part-singing
- Instrumental: Technique, notation, ensembles
- Literacy: Notation, composition, improvisation

## Musical Contexts
Music history, periods, composers, world music, American music

# Mathematics

## Core Domains
- **Number & Operations:** Counting, place value, operations, fractions, decimals
- **Algebra:** Patterns, equations, functions, proportional reasoning
- **Geometry & Measurement:** Shapes, attributes, transformations, measurement
- **Statistics & Probability:** Data, graphs, likelihood

## Mathematical Practices
1. Make sense of problems and persevere
2. Reason abstractly and quantitatively
3. Construct arguments and critique reasoning
4. Model with mathematics
5. Use appropriate tools strategically
6. Attend to precision
7. Look for and use structure
8. Express regularity in repeated reasoning

# English Language Arts

## Core Components
- **Reading:** Foundational skills, literature, informational, fluency, comprehension
- **Writing:** Types (narrative, informational, opinion), process, conventions
- **Speaking & Listening:** Comprehension, collaboration, presentation
- **Language:** Conventions, vocabulary acquisition

## Text Complexity
- Quantitative measures (lexile, readability)
- Qualitative measures (structure, language, knowledge demands)
- Reader and task considerations

# Visual Arts

## Elements of Art
Line, Shape, Form, Color, Texture, Value, Space

## Principles of Design
Balance, Emphasis, Movement, Pattern, Rhythm, Unity, Proportion

## Media & Techniques
- Drawing: Pencil, charcoal, pastel, ink
- Painting: Watercolor, tempera, acrylic
- Sculpture: Clay, found materials
- Printmaking: Relief, stamping
- Collage and mixed media

## Art Appreciation
Periods, styles, artists, cultural contexts, critique

# Science

## Core Domains
- **Physical Science:** Matter, forces, motion, energy, waves
- **Life Science:** Molecules to organisms, ecosystems, heredity, evolution
- **Earth & Space:** Earth's place, systems, human impact
- **Engineering:** Design process, technology connections

## Science & Engineering Practices
- Asking questions, defining problems
- Developing and using models
- Planning and carrying out investigations
- Analyzing and interpreting data
- Using mathematics and computational thinking
- Constructing explanations, designing solutions
- Engaging in argument from evidence
- Obtaining, evaluating, communicating information

# Health Education

## Personal Health & Wellness
- **Physical:** Nutrition, activity, sleep, hygiene
- **Mental/Emotional:** Emotions, stress management, self-esteem, mental health awareness
- **Social:** Relationships, conflict resolution, peer pressure, bullying prevention

## Safety & Prevention
- Personal safety, emergency procedures, internet safety
- Substance abuse prevention, peer influence

## Growth & Development
- Body knowledge, puberty, reproduction, family diversity

## Community Health
Community resources, environmental health

# Advanced Level

Deep understanding, abstract reasoning, synthesis.

## Targets
- Analyze complex problems and situations
- Evaluate arguments and evidence
- Create original solutions or products
- Transfer learning to unfamiliar contexts
- Synthesize information from multiple sources

## Support Needs
- Minimal scaffolding, consultation when needed
- Authentic, real-world challenges

# Extension Level

Beyond grade-level, expert-like understanding.

## Targets
- Novel applications and creations
- Transfer across domains
- Critique and refinement of work
- Development of expertise

## Support Needs
- Expert guidance and feedback
- Access to professional resources
- Time for deep exploration

# Proficient Level

Solid understanding, independent application.

## Targets
- Apply skills to novel situations
- Explain thinking with clear reasoning
- Make connections across concepts
- Choose appropriate strategies
- Solve multi-step problems

## Support Needs
- Extended challenges and enrichment
- Choice in approach and representation

# Developing Level

Building understanding, moving toward independence.

## Targets
- Apply skills with increasing independence
- Explain reasoning and processes
- Recognize patterns and relationships
- Solve familiar problems

## Support Needs
- Strategic prompting and cues
- Partially completed examples
- Graphic organizers and visual supports

# Introduction Level

First exposure to content. Concrete, tangible examples.

## Targets
- Recognize and identify basic concepts
- Understand key vocabulary
- Follow simple procedures with support
- Make connections to prior knowledge

## Support Needs
- Clear step-by-step instructions
- Scaffolding and guided practice
- Multiple examples and models

config.label: Curriculum Standards
config.description: Curriculum frameworks, standards alignment, and learning objectives

config.label: Current Unit or Topic Focus
config.description: Specific content focus for this lesson/unit
config.placeholder: e.g., Fractions, Civil Rights Movement, Photosynthesis...

children.content:
# Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS)

## Three Dimensions
1. **Disciplinary Core Ideas:** Physical, Life, Earth/Space Science, Engineering
2. **Science & Engineering Practices:** Questioning, modeling, investigating, analyzing, arguing
3. **Crosscutting Concepts:** Patterns, cause/effect, systems, energy/matter, structure/function

Performance expectations integrate all three dimensions

# International Baccalaureate (IB)

## Programmes
- **PYP (ages 3-12):** Transdisciplinary, learner profile focus, exhibition
- **MYP (ages 11-16):** Subject groups, global contexts, personal project
- **DP (ages 16-19):** Diploma programme, university recognition

## Key Features
- Concept-based curriculum
- International-mindedness
- Approaches to teaching and learning skills

# State-Specific Standards

States that developed their own standards (not CCSS).

Examples:
- **Virginia:** Standards of Learning (SOL)
- **Minnesota:** Academic Standards
- Other individual state standards

Vary in organization and emphasis from national models

# Cambridge International Curriculum

## Pathway
- **Primary:** Ages 5-11
- **Lower Secondary:** Ages 11-14
- **Upper Secondary:** IGCSE, ages 14-16
- **Advanced:** AS & A Levels, ages 16-19

## Key Features
- Internationally recognized qualifications
- Clear progression across stages
- Skills development: critical thinking, problem-solving
- University acceptance worldwide

# Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills (TEKS)

- **State Standards:** Texas-specific K-12 curriculum
- **All Subjects:** Comprehensive across all content areas
- **STAAR Alignment:** State assessment aligned to TEKS

**Key Features:**
- Readiness Standards (essential for success)
- Supporting Standards (introduced, not essential)
- Process Standards (ways of thinking within each discipline)

# Common Core State Standards (CCSS)

## English Language Arts
- **Key Shifts:** Content-rich nonfiction, evidence-based reading/writing, complex text
- **Strands:** Reading, Writing, Speaking/Listening, Language
- **Literacy:** History/Science technical subjects included

## Mathematics
- **Key Shifts:** Focus, coherence, rigor (conceptual understanding, procedural skill, application)
- **Domains:** Operations, algebra, geometry, measurement, data, fractions, ratios, functions, statistics

Focus on college and career readiness

# Summative Assessment

**Assessment OF Learning** - evaluates learning at the end of instruction

## Types
- End-of-unit tests and projects
- Final exams
- Standardized assessments
- Performance tasks
- Portfolios (cumulative)

## Purpose
- Determine mastery of standards
- Assign grades for reporting
- Evaluate program effectiveness

# Standardized Testing

Uniform assessment administered to all students under same conditions.

## Types
- State accountability assessments
- District benchmarks
- Nationally normed tests (Iowa, Stanford)
- College entrance exams (SAT, ACT)
- English proficiency assessments (ACCESS)

## Purpose
- Comparability across students/schools
- Accountability and program evaluation

# Project-Based Assessment

Students complete meaningful, real-world projects.

## Characteristics
- Authentic, real-world context
- Requires higher-order thinking
- Extended time to complete
- Public product or presentation

## Assesses
- Process as well as product
- Collaboration and communication
- Content knowledge application

frontmatter.title: Portfolio-Based
frontmatter.description: Collection of student work over time

children.content:
# Portfolio-Based Assessment

Collection of work gathered over time showing growth and achievement.

## Types
- **Growth Portfolio:** Shows progress and development
- **Showcase Portfolio:** Best work demonstration
- **Process Portfolio:** Documents learning process

## Advantages
- Shows growth over time
- Captures complexity of learning
- Student ownership and agency

# Performance Tasks

Students apply knowledge and skills in authentic tasks.

## Characteristics
- Real-world context
- Multiple steps and solution paths
- Extended time to complete
- Clear evaluation criteria

## Examples
- Science lab investigations
- Mathematical problem-solving tasks
- Writing prompts with research
- Speaking and listening demonstrations

# Formative Assessment

**Assessment FOR Learning** - ongoing checks to guide instruction

## Strategies
- Exit tickets and quick checks
- Think-pair-share and discussion
- Whiteboard responses
- Observation during work
- Questioning and conferences

## Purpose
- Identify who understands and who needs help
- Guide instructional next steps
- Provide feedback to students
